name: ringtailkeys
description: The 4th bucket, worked as a CLI: `ringtail`, the terminal you (or your coding agent) drive. Reads the project's .env.example manifest via @ringtail/core and prints the provisioning plan; `ringtail up` boots the BUNDLED daemon (dist/daemon.js) + serves the prebuilt dashboard + MCP on one origin; `--json` is agent mode (emits what's MISSING, never secret values). Published as `ringtailkeys` with bin `ringtail`. bun bundles src/cli.ts → dist/cli.js AND services/daemon → dist/daemon.js (esbuild HANGS on this machine — bun only). type:package → imports libs only (@ringtail/core, @ringtail/config); terminal — nothing internal consumes it.

scripts.build: bun build src/cli.ts --target=bun --outfile dist/cli.js && chmod +x dist/cli.js
scripts.build:dashboard: (cd ../../apps/dashboard && bun run build) && rm -rf dist/dashboard && cp -r ../../apps/dashboard/dist dist/dashboard
scripts.prepack: bun run build && bun run build:daemon && bun run build:dashboard

$publishConfig_comment: No `private` flag: this package IS meant to be published. Accidental publishes are prevented not by `private` but by (a) the Tilt `release` button's clean-tree + on-master preflight and (b) publish.yml's guard that the pushed v* tag must equal this version. Publishing runs ONLY in CI via OIDC Trusted Publishing (no npm token anywhere) — press the Tilt `release` button → it bumps+tags+pushes → the tag fires publish.yml → `npm publish --provenance --access public`. Ships `dist/` (cli.js + daemon.js + dashboard/) as `ringtailkeys`, installing the `ringtail` bin. `types` points at source; a real publish adds `tsc --emitDeclarationOnly` for dist/index.d.ts if a consumer ever needs it.
